How to use the `git submodule` command
============================================

The `git submodule` command is used to manage submodules in a Git repository. A submodule is a Git repository that is included in another Git repository as a separate module. Submodules are useful when you want to manage a project that depends on another project, but you don't want to include the entire project in your repository.

In this guide, we will cover the basics of using the `git submodule` command, including how to create and manage submodules, and how to update them.

### Creating a Submodule

To create a submodule in a Git repository, you can use the following command:
```
git submodule add <repository-url>
```
This command will add a new submodule to your repository, and initialize it with the specified repository. The `<repository-url>` parameter should be the URL of the repository you want to add as a submodule.
